preview_text:
The model identifies Belgium as a modest favorite with a 38% win probability, but the margin over Iran is narrow—only 8 percentage points separate them. Iran's 30% chance reflects a competitive proposition rather than an outsider role.

The draw at 32% sits substantially above either team's individual win odds, suggesting the model expects a tightly contested match where a stalemate is nearly as likely as a Belgium victory. The three-way spread is relatively flat, indicating genuine uncertainty across all outcomes.

The model and market diverge sharply. Sportsbooks assign Belgium 68% and Iran just 12%, a gap of 56 percentage points in Belgium's favor compared to the model's 8-point spread. Polymarket aligns closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the disagreement reflects fundamentally different readings of the matchup's competitive balance.
